Cyber Operate - Oracle Access Governance (OAG)  - Manager -  - 326348 Skip to content Deloitte | India Offices of the US Menu Home Jobs Entry level Experienced Events Login Cyber Operate - Oracle Access Governance (OAG)  - Manager Same job available in 4 locations Bengaluru, Karnataka, India Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India Hyderabad, Telangana, India Pune, Maharashtra, India Back to search results Apply Now Share this job: Share: Share Share Cyber Operate - Oracle Access Governance (OAG)  - Manager with Facebook Share Cyber Operate - Oracle Access Governance (OAG)  - Manager with LinkedIn Share Cyber Operate - Oracle Access Governance (OAG)  - Manager with Twitter Share Cyber Operate - Oracle Access Governance (OAG)  - Manager with a friend via e-mail Back to search results Position Summary Cyber Deloitte Cyber understands the unique challenges and opportunities businesses face in cybersecurity. Join our team to deliver powerful insights to help our clients navigate the ever-changing threat design, and technology as we partner with clients to transform finance. Oracle Access Governance Qualifications: Work you’ll do Partner with IAM stakeholders to      intake, clarify, and document run-state requirements for Oracle Access      Governance (OAG) (incidents, service requests, access campaign changes,      onboarding requests, enhancements). Operate and continuously improve      OAG configurations to ensure scalability, security, and compliance, while      leveraging prior implementation experience to design durable patterns. Build and support      integrations/connectors between OAG and enterprise applications,      directories, and cloud platforms; monitor connector health, job schedules,      and data quality. Configure and maintain OAG      workflows, rules, and lifecycle triggers to automate identity governance      processes, including provisioning, deprovisioning, and access      certifications. Operate policy and control      enforcement within OAG: policy-based access, Segregation of Duties (SOD)      controls, guardrails, and exception handling; support periodic control      testing and audits. Provide senior-level technical      oversight and mentorship to teams executing changes; review      configurations, promote standards, and reduce operational risk (e.g.,      change/release hygiene, rollback plans). Troubleshoot and resolve complex      OAG issues (performance, integration failures, identity      correlation/matching, certification anomalies, entitlement/role mapping      problems); perform root-cause analysis and drive permanent fixes. Ensure OAG operations align to      security best practices and compliance requirements (e.g., SOX, HIPAA,      GDPR), including audit evidence and traceability for approvals and access      decisions. Create and maintain      runbooks/SOPs, design/configuration documentation, and operational      procedures (monitoring, alert triage, job recovery, campaign execution      playbooks). Required skills Hands-on experience operating an      identity governance platform, Oracle Access Governance (OAG), including      access certifications, policies, and lifecycle operations; implementation      experience strongly preferred. Experience with OAG building      blocks such as Access Bundles, Roles, Access Guardrails, and workflows,      and operating these in production with controlled change management. Strong understanding of RBAC      (Role-Based Access Control), user lifecycle management, access      review/certification execution, and SOD concepts and remediation patterns. Integration experience with      enterprise apps/directories and exposure to IT Service Management (ITSM)      tool integration for request/approval flows. Basic understanding of Oracle      Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) concepts (helpful for cloud app onboarding and      integration patterns). Strong troubleshooting,      documentation, and stakeholder management skills in an operate model      (SLAs, prioritization, and cross-team coordination). Excellent communication skills;      able to create and maintain SOPs, runbooks, and audit-ready documentation. Solid project      management/organizational skills in an operate model (prioritization,      SLAs, release coordination, and change management) #Cyber_Cyber Operate Our purpose Deloitte’s purpose is to make an impact that matters for our people, clients, and communities. At Deloitte, purpose is synonymous with how we work every day. It defines who we are. Our purpose comes through in our work with clients that enables impact and value in their organizations, as well as through our own investments, commitments, and actions across areas that help drive positive outcomes for our communities. Our people and culture Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ways of thinking, ideas and perspectives, and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients’ most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work. Professional development At Deloitte, professionals have the opportunity to work with some of the best and discover what works best for them. Here, we prioritize professional growth, offering diverse learning and networking opportunities to help accelerate careers and enhance leadership skills. Our state-of-the-art DU: The Leadership Center in India, located in Hyderabad, represents a tangible symbol of our commitment to the holistic growth and development of our people. Explore DU: The Leadership Center in India . Benefits to help you thrive At Deloitte, we know that great people make a great organization. Our comprehensive rewards program helps us deliver a distinctly Deloitte experience that helps that empowers our professionals to thrive mentally, physically, and financially—and live their purpose. To support our professionals and their loved ones, we offer a broad range of benefits. Eligibility requirements may be based on role, tenure, type of employment and/ or other criteria. Learn more about what working at Deloitte can mean for you. Deloitte refers to one or more of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, a UK private company limited by guarantee ("DTTL"), its network of member firms, and their related entities. DTTL and each of its member firms are legally separate and independent entities. DTTL (also referred to as "Deloitte Global") does not provide services to clients. In the United States, Deloitte refers to one or more of the US member firms of DTTL, their related entities that operate using the "Deloitte" name in the United States and their respective affiliates. Certain services may not be available to attest clients under the rules and regulations of public accounting. Please see www.deloitte.com/about to learn more about our global network of member firms.
